reasons: Bible cap treated as 30% of 80k target (absolute), not 30% of actual final train tokens
Bible 91.9% of train rows — hard cap violation
Hoʻoilina promoted to dev — Rusty §1.4 violation; promoted rows must never enter dev/test
Bible cap computed pre-HK against (T_nonbible_total + T_bible_kept) reference, NOT against actual final train tokens
After HK was capped, T_final_train shrank; actual Bible share rose to 64.8% and HK to 32.4%
Cap math self-consistent against its own denominator but VIOLATES the stated <=30% / <=15% on the artifact itself

fixed_point_solution.derivation: S/T<=0.15, B/T<=0.30, H/T<=0.15. Select software-l10n first with S_max=3N/8, then set M=N+S and solve H_max=3M/11, B_max=6M/11.

bible_cap.algorithm: Pool 1839-train + 1868-net-new-quality + john-1854-verse-dedup; sort sha256_pair asc; greedy <= B_target; verify against artifact and drop tail until <=30%

hk_cap.algorithm: Filter by Rusty §1.5; sort sha256_pair asc; greedy <= H_target; verify against artifact

software_l10n_cap.algorithm: Filter permissive-license Weblate PO rows; sort sha256_pair asc; greedy <= S_target; verify against artifact

blockers: allenai/nllb lang-pair table has no haw_Latn entry.
datasets-server haw_Latn-eng_Latn and eng_Latn-haw_Latn configs return 404.
Do not substitute hau_Latn (Hausa) or hat_Latn (Haitian Creole) for Hawaiian.
alignment_method=labse required (LaBSE/LASER not available in this repo).
Comparable-not-parallel: pairs cannot be ingested into stage2_manifest.jsonl as train-ready until a scored alignment pass exists.
Wikimedia ToS = CC BY-SA 4.0 / GFDL: prototype_only=true, release_eligible=false until rights review confirms attribution carry-through.
alignment_method=labse required; LaBSE/LASER not wired in this repo.
Comparable paired volumes: chapter-level pairing is plausible after OCR repair, but paragraph/sentence pairing is not deterministic because paragraph counts diverge.
Do not emit review-pending sentence rows until a scored alignment pass exists.
Plan endpoint https://haw.wikisource.org/w/api.php redirects to multilingual wikisource.org and returns HTML, not a haw-specific API JSON endpoint.
A replacement adapter must target multilingual Wikisource explicitly and define a defensible Hawaiian-page enumeration strategy before fetching full text.
Even after endpoint repair, alignment_method=labse is required before sentence candidates can be emitted.
Hard-escalate cultural categories must be filtered before any future full-text pull.

notes: Caps verified directly against the emitted artifact, not a reference denominator.
Frozen Tatoeba dev (15 rows) untouched. No promoted review-pending row enters dev/test.
Hoʻoilina: 60 sentence-level rows promoted to train (prototype-only); 68 paragraph-level rows remain deferred (future-work-native-review).

Phrase Book 1881 (Bishop 4th ed., U.S. PD): 2516 phrase-pair rows promoted to train (release-eligible, NOT prototype-only); uncapped non-Bible/non-HK contributor to N. Adapter at scripts/328_build_phrase_book_candidates.py.
1850/1869 HK pair stays inventory-only per Linus year-mismatch flag.
Bible 1839 historical-orthography accepted rows that exceed cap are quarantined to review-pending with 'dropped-by-bible-cap-v2-fixedpoint'; the canonical manifest is unchanged.
SFT emitter requires --allow-review-required because HK 1897 and Hoʻoilina sentence rows carry alignment_review_required=true at ingestion despite passing their promotion rules.
N denominator now includes Hoʻoilina sentence + Phrase Book 1881 train tokens, slightly increasing Bible/HK cap budgets.
Weblate permissive-license software-l10n rows: 103 promoted under a <=15% software-l10n token cap; 0 cap-held and 4 quality-held.
OPUS Wikimedia source-line-aligned rows: 275 scorer-accepted rows promoted; remaining OPUS rows hard-held with concrete verdicts.
Wikimedia CX high-human/no-MT rows: 2 promoted; 12 held out under the conservative CX train gate.
This is NOT a path to 80k by review-pending promotion alone; NLLB-mined and synthetic BT remain required (per Rusty §4 and Linus ulukau-sft-vetting).
